expectations.text: Code resolves FrameDebuggerUtility and FrameDebuggerEventData types via Assembly.GetType in static constructor
expectations.evidence: Lines 213-216: s_UtilType = editorAssembly.GetType("UnityEditorInternal.FrameDebuggerInternal.FrameDebuggerUtility"); s_EventDataType = editorAssembly.GetType("UnityEditorInternal.FrameDebuggerInternal.FrameDebuggerEventData"); — both resolved via Assembly.GetType inside the static constructor (lines 207-313).

expectations.text: Uses Delegate.CreateDelegate for static method/property access (not MethodInfo.Invoke for simple methods)
expectations.evidence: Lines 235-249: All static methods (SetEnabled, GetRemotePlayerGUID, GetFrameEvents, GetFrameEventInfoName, GetFrameEventObject) and properties (count, limit) use CreateStaticDelegate/CreatePropertyGetter/CreatePropertySetter, which internally call Delegate.CreateDelegate (see lines 879-941). MethodInfo.Invoke is only used for GetFrameEventData (line 251-254 comment: "internal param type — must use MethodInfo.Invoke").

expectations.text: Contains SetEnabled(bool, int) method wrapping the FrameDebuggerUtility.SetEnabled call
expectations.evidence: Lines 322-325: public static void SetEnabled(bool enable, int guid) { s_SetEnabled?.Invoke(enable, guid); } — wraps the delegate bound to FrameDebuggerUtility.SetEnabled.

expectations.text: Contains StartCapture with EditorApplication.update-based async capture loop
expectations.evidence: Lines 806-873: public static void StartCapture(Action<List<FrameDebuggerRawEvent>> onDone) { ... void Tick() { ... } EditorApplication.update += Tick; } — uses EditorApplication.update for async per-frame capture of all events.

expectations.text: Extracts shaderName/passName/passIndex/shaderKeywords from FrameDebuggerEventData via reflection field accessors
expectations.evidence: Lines 469-519: GetOriginalShaderName (line 469-472) extracts shaderName, GetPassName (481-484) extracts passName, GetShaderPassIndex (509-514) extracts passIndex, GetShaderKeywords (517-520) extracts shaderKeywords — all via FieldInfo.GetValue on FrameDebuggerEventData instance fields.

expectations.text: FrameDebuggerRawEvent data class includes index, shaderName, passIndex, passName, lightMode, shaderKeywords fields
expectations.evidence: Lines 14-78: FrameDebuggerRawEvent class contains public int index (line 17), public string shaderName (27), public int passIndex (38), public string passName (41), public string lightMode (44), public string shaderKeywords (50) — all six fields present.

expectations.text: Uses null-conditional operators (?.) or explicit null checks on reflection delegates/field accessors
expectations.evidence: Line 324: s_SetEnabled?.Invoke(enable, guid); — null-conditional operator. Line 389: s_GetFrameEvents?.Invoke(); — null-conditional. Line 471: s_FI_OriginalShaderName?.GetValue(data) as string ?? string.Empty; — null-conditional. Line 511: if (data == null || s_FI_ShaderPassIndex == null) return -1; — explicit null checks. These patterns appear consistently throughout the file.

expectations.text: Uses Activator.CreateInstance with 'true' parameter for internal type construction
expectations.evidence: Line 456: return Activator.CreateInstance(s_EventDataType, nonPublic: true); — uses Activator.CreateInstance with nonPublic: true to construct the internal FrameDebuggerEventData type.

eval_feedback.overall: All 8 expectations are met with clear, direct evidence in the output. The code demonstrates proper reflection-based FrameDebugger access using Assembly.GetType, Delegate.CreateDelegate for static members, EditorApplication.update-based async capture, field-level extraction of shader/pass/keyword data, and proper null safety. The implementation is thorough and well-structured.
